nodes.name: Execute Input Command
nodes.notes: Runs the command string from $json.input in the OS shell and returns stdout/stderr. Output is passed back to the calling workflow as the sub-workflow result.

nodes.name: When Executed by Another Workflow
nodes.notes: Entry point for sub-workflow calls. Expects one input field: input (string) - the full shell command to run (e.g. kubectl get pods -A).

parameters.content:
## 🔧 Input Command Workflow

A **utility sub-workflow** used as a tool by AI agent workflows. It receives a shell command string and executes it on the n8n host, returning the output.

**Used by:**
- Homelab AI — Kubernetes Health Monitor
- K8s Health AI Workflow
- K8s RCA AI Workflow
- K8s Remediation AI Workflow

**How it works:**
1. Parent workflow calls this sub-workflow with an `input` field (the command string)
2. This workflow runs the command via the OS shell
3. Output (stdout/stderr) is returned to the calling workflow

## ⚙️ Setup Requirements

**kubectl** and **talosctl** must be installed and configured on the machine running n8n.

- `~/.kube/config` must point to your cluster
- `talosctl` endpoints must be reachable
- n8n process must have permission to execute these binaries

**Security note:** This sub-workflow executes arbitrary shell commands. Only connect it to trusted AI agents with a read-only system prompt.
